enonce.texte: Lisez les phrases suivantes et identifiez si elles sont polies (polite) ou impolies (impolite).

questions.texte: 'Give me your phone!'
'Could you please help me with this?'
'Would you mind waiting for a moment?'
'What do you want?'
'Please hold on, I'll be right back.'

intitule: Vocabulaire de la politesse au téléphone

enonce.texte: Complétez les phrases avec les mots de la liste suivante : please, thank you, excuse me, hold on, could you, would you, sorry, welcome

questions.texte: ______ help me with this problem?
______, may I speak to Mr. Ndi?
______, the line is busy. I'll call back later.
______ a moment, please. I'll check for you.
______ you mind repeating that, please?

enonce.texte:
Lisez le dialogue suivant et répondez aux questions.

Mary: Hello, may I speak to Mr. John, please?

Receptionist: Certainly. Could you please hold on for a moment? I'll check if he is available.

Mary: Thank you very much.

Receptionist: I'm sorry, but Mr. John is in a meeting right now. Would you like to leave a message?

Mary: Yes, please. Could you tell him that Mary called? I will call back later.

Receptionist: Of course. I'll give him your message.

Mary: Thank you for your help. Goodbye.

Receptionist: You're welcome. Goodbye.

questions.texte: Who does Mary want to speak to?
What does the receptionist ask Mary to do?
questions.reponse: She asks Mary to hold on.

questions.texte: Why can't Mary speak to Mr. John?
questions.reponse: Because Mr. John is in a meeting.

questions.texte: What message does Mary leave?
questions.reponse: She says that Mary called and she will call back later.

enonce.texte: Complétez les phrases avec la forme en -ing des verbes entre parenthèses.

questions.texte: ______ (use) a phone requires good manners.
______ (send) a message is very quick.
______ (speak) politely on the internet is important.
______ (write) emails is a useful skill.
______ (chat) with friends online is fun.

enonce.texte: Remettez les phrases suivantes dans l'ordre pour former un dialogue cohérent.

questions.texte:
A. 'Goodbye, take care.'
B. 'Yes, please. Thank you for your help.'
C. 'Hello, could I speak to the manager, please?'
D. 'Certainly. Would you like to leave a message?'
E. 'I'm sorry, but the manager is not available at the moment.'

intitule: Rédiger un dialogue poli au téléphone

enonce.texte: Rédigez un dialogue entre deux personnes au téléphone. Utilisez les expressions de politesse que vous avez apprises.

questions.texte: Rédigez un dialogue de 8 à 10 répliques au téléphone.
questions.reponse: Critères d'évaluation : - Utilisation des expressions de politesse (please, could you, would you, hold on, etc.) - Structure d'un dialogue - Qualité de la langue anglaise.

sousQuestions.texte: Exemple : A: 'Hello, may I speak to Mr. Ndi?' B: 'Certainly. Please hold on...'
sousQuestions.reponse: Le dialogue sera évalué sur la qualité.

intitule: QCM sur la politesse au téléphone et sur internet

enonce.texte: Choisissez la bonne réponse pour chaque question.

questions.texte: Que dit-on pour demander poliment quelque chose ?
Que dit-on pour demander à quelqu'un d'attendre au téléphone ?
Que signifie 'Would you mind...?'
Quelle phrase est la plus polie pour terminer un appel ?
questions.reponse: c) Thank you for your help. Goodbye.

sousQuestions.texte: c) Thank you for your help. Goodbye.

intitule: Rédiger un message poli sur internet

enonce.texte: Rédigez un message poli que vous enverriez à un ami ou à un enseignant sur WhatsApp ou par email.

questions.texte: Rédigez un message poli sur internet.
questions.reponse: Critères d'évaluation : - Utilisation des expressions de politesse - Adaptation au format (message court) - Qualité de la langue anglaise.

sousQuestions.texte: Exemple : 'Hello, I hope you are well. Could you please help me with my homework? I need help with the English exercise. Thank you very much for your time.'
sousQuestions.reponse: Le message sera évalué sur la qualité.

intitule: Transformer des phrases impolies en phrases polies

enonce.texte: Transformez les phrases suivantes en utilisant des expressions de politesse.

questions.texte: 'Give me your phone number.'
questions.reponse: Could you please give me your phone number? / Would you give me your phone number, please?

questions.texte: 'Wait. I'll call you later.'
questions.reponse: Please hold on. I'll call you back later.

questions.texte: 'Send me the information.'
questions.reponse: Could you please send me the information? / Would you mind sending me the information?

questions.texte: 'Tell me the answer.'
questions.reponse: Could you please tell me the answer? / Would you mind telling me the answer?
